l	Z	d
d!d"
Z'd#d$
Z(e)d%k
d&l*m+Z+
e+d'd(d)
RPC Implementation, originally written for the Python Idle IDE
For security reasons, GvR requested that Idle's Python execution server process
connect to the Idle process, which listens for the connection.  Since Idle has
only one client per server, this was not a limitation.
   +---------------------------------+ +-------------+
   | socketserver.BaseRequestHandler | | SocketIO    |
   +---------------------------------+ +-------------+
                   ^                   | register()  |
                   |                   | unregister()|
                   |                   +-------------+
                   |                      ^  ^
                   |                      |  |
                   | + -------------------+  |
                   | |                       |
   +-------------------------+        +-----------------+
   | RPCHandler              |        | RPCClient       |
   | [attribute of RPCServer]|        |                 |
   +-------------------------+        +-----------------+
The RPCServer handler class is expected to provide register/unregister methods.
RPCHandler inherits the mix-in class SocketIO, which provides these methods.
See the Idle run.main() docstring for further information on how this was
accomplished in Idle.
z*Return code object from marshal string ms.)
marshal
loads)
#/usr/lib64/python3.9/idlelib/rpc.py

Override TCPServer method, connect() instead of listen()
        Due to the reversed connection, self.server_address is actually the
        address of the Idle Client to which we are connecting.

        Error message goes to __stderr__.  No error message if exiting
        normally or socket raised EOF.  Other exceptions not handled in
        server code will cause os._exit.

-----------------------r(
cannot unpickle packet:)
UnpicklingErrorr-
print_stack)
pollmessage
SocketIO.pollmessagec
}	|	d
Handle messages received on the socket.
        Some messages received may be asynchronous 'call' or 'queue' requests,
        and some may be responses for other threads.
        'call' requests are passed to self.localcall() with the expectation of
        immediate execution, during which time the socket is not serviced.
        'queue' requests are used for tasks (which may block or hang) to be
        processed in a different thread.  These requests are fed into
        request_queue by self.localcall().  Responses to queued requests are
        taken from response_queue and sent across the link with the associated
        sequence numbers.  Messages in the queues are (sequence_number,
        request/response) tuples and code using this module removing messages
        from the request_queue is responsible for returning the correct
        sequence number in the response_queue.
        pollresponse() will loop until a response message with the myseq
        sequence number is received, and will save other responses in
        self.responses and notify the owning thread.
        r
pollresponse:%d:myseq:%s)
pollresponse:%d:localcall:call:z%pollresponse:%d:localcall:response:%srS
response_queue
queueZ
Emptyru
handle_EOFr
notifyr
qmsgrd
resqre
SocketIO.pollresponsec
z+action taken upon link being closed by peerr
EOFhookrH
keyr
SocketIO.handle_EOFc
zBClasses using rpc client/server can override to augment EOF actionNr
SocketIO.EOFhook)
NN)"r
